stories.title: Spain's Fabian Ruiz, Gavi Get Their Weight In Tomatoes After World Cup Win
stories.summary: Getting showered with tomatoes after a performance is usually a bad thing. But in Spain, it's a sign of ultimate respect for two players in its World Cup-winning squad. Fabián Ruiz and Gavi were presented with their weight in tomatoes by their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca in the Andalusia region, located in the south of Spain. The two players joined former Spain great Jesus Navas (part of the 2010 World Cup squad) at the town's city hall as part of the ongoing revelry to celebrate La Roja's victory over Argentina in the final on Sunday. Andalusia is known for its agriculture and production of tomatoes.

stories.title: The night Lionel Messi got a jersey from Texas A&M QB Marcel Reed
stories.summary: Lionel Messi received a signed Texas A&M jersey from quarterback Marcel Reed during an exhibition match against Honduras on June 6, 2023, at Kyle Field, which drew over 90,000 fans. The brief exchange occurred after Argentina's 2-0 victory, with Reed also obtaining a signed ball from Messi. This moment highlights Messi's continued influence in sports, even as he prepares for a break following the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where Argentina faced a heartbreaking loss in the final.

stories.title: Modeling agent who introduced women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old modeling agent linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, France, on Monday. French prosecutors have opened an investigation into the cause of death, with an autopsy scheduled to be conducted. Siad was under scrutiny for allegedly facilitating Epstein's trafficking of young women and had denied any wrongdoing prior to his death. His name appeared in over 1,000 documents related to Epstein's case, raising significant concerns about his involvement.

stories.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
stories.summary: Four juveniles face court in Virginia Thursday after a serious public order incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, left three people with significant but non-life-threatening injuries. Police arrested the teens following searches near Lough Ramor after reports of a weapon being produced. The minors will appear before Cavan District Children's Court, with media barred from naming them. Investigations continue.

stories.title: The video of the Argentina national team in the tunnel that buries the conspiracy theories about the defeat against Spain
stories.summary: Argentina released unseen tunnel footage showing Messi rallying teammates before their 2026 World Cup final loss to Spain, dispelling online conspiracy theories about prearranged outcomes. The three-minute video, filmed at MetLife Stadium on July 19, 2026, captures Messi urging calm amid pressure. Meanwhile, Argentina marked the 30th anniversary of the 1994 AMIA bombing that killed 85, with U.S. lawmakers renewing calls for justice. Messi’s World Cup performance keeps him in Ballon d’Or contention through July 31.

stories.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
stories.summary: Democracy Dies in Darkness Jasper Philipsen has capped a fine team performance to win stage 17 of the Tour de France, where defending champion Tadej Pogačar protected his overall lead July 22, 2026 at 1:33 p.m. EDT 4 minutes ago Belgium’s Jasper Philipsen reacts after winning the seventeenth stage of the Tour de France cycling race, with start in Chambery and finish in Voiron France, Wednesday, July 22, 2026. (Gonzalo Fuentes/Pool Photo via AP) By Associated Press VOIRON, France — Jasper Philipsen capped a fine team performance Wednesday to win stage 17 of the Tour de France , where defending champion Tadej Pogačar protected his overall lead.

stories.title: Aston Villa agree deal to sign Chelsea forward Alejandro Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa has secured a deal to sign Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho on an initial season-long loan, with a conditional obligation to make the transfer permanent. The 22-year-old has completed his medical and is finalizing paperwork for a four-year contract. Villa manager Unai Emery, a long-time admirer of Garnacho, aims to bolster his squad following Chelsea's record £117 million signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa. If certain conditions are met, the deal could become permanent by 2027.

stories.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
stories.summary: ResilExec Coaching has launched two new programs, Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™, aimed at mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. These initiatives complete a comprehensive career advancement platform, addressing critical moments where leaders often lose momentum, such as external transitions and post-advancement challenges. Founder Brian Baldari emphasizes that the programs will help leaders navigate the complexities of market re-entry and long-term career execution. The launch signifies a significant expansion of ResilExec's offerings to support professionals facing career shifts.

stories.title: No Garnacho for Roma & Napoli as Argentine joins Aston Villa on loan
stories.summary: Alejandro Garnacho is set to join Aston Villa on loan from Chelsea, with a conditional obligation to buy that could make the deal permanent in 2027. The agreement comes after interest from Serie A clubs Roma and Napoli, which was hindered by Garnacho's wage demands. Villa manager Unai Emery played a crucial role in the move, as the club aims to bolster its attacking options. Garnacho has already completed his medical and is finalizing paperwork in Birmingham.

stories.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
stories.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as they target a summer signing. The France international, who played one World Cup game, scored seven goals and made 11 assists last season. PSG have also bid for him, with Monaco rejecting their latest offer around £34 million. Liverpool are expected to make more additions before the transfer window closes.

stories.title: Forest Green Rovers make double swoop as midfielder and attacker join
stories.summary: Forest Green Rovers have made a double signing with forward Omari Patrick arriving on a permanent deal and midfielder Kyle Kelly on a season-long loan from Liverpool. Kelly, 20, has been in Liverpool's age-grade set-up since 2018, most recently playing for their under-21s team. The youngster also has eight caps fro St Kitts and Nevis, scoring once, during a friendly in March against the Solomon Islands. The loan is for an initial six months, and has left boss Robbie Savage delighted to have got his man. "What a great guy," he said. "Very excited to have Kyle join us at FGR.

stories.title: Cape Verde hero Vozinha features in FIFA's Best XI from World Cup, with three World Champions from Spain
stories.summary: FIFA has released the official World Cup Best XI, the best players chosen (voted by fans) for every position after the competition ended. Spain, world champions, feature three players, and Argentina, runner-ups, feature two.What surprised some is that France, who ended in fourth place after a thrilling 6-4 defeat to England in the third-place play-off, also has three players; while England only one (and is not Harry Kane).

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 - Schedule, draw and prize money
stories.summary: The 2026 World Matchplay Darts begins Saturday, July 18 at Blackpool’s Winter Gardens with defending champion Luke Littler leading the 32-player field. The PDC major event runs through July 26, including a women’s final on the last day where Lisa Ashton defends her title. Littler, the world number one, has already claimed three majors this year. The tournament features seeded draws to separate top-ranked players early on.

stories.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
stories.summary: New Jersey officials reported a software error registering over 6,600 non-U.S. citizens as voters ahead of the 2024 presidential election. Governor Mikie Sherrill called the mistake unacceptable and said fewer than 400 actually cast ballots. The error occurred between June 2023 and June 2024 during driver’s license and ID applications. State officials are reviewing safeguards ahead of upcoming elections.

stories.title: Text of new Gordie Howe bridge deal seems to contradict Carney
stories.summary: Canada will open the $4.7 billion Gordie Howe International Bridge on July 27, but a planned joint U.S.-Canada ceremony on July 24 has been canceled amid rising trade tensions. Ottawa released the text of a controversial revenue-sharing deal, promising to split net bridge profits with a U.S. economic fund for 15 years. President Trump’s new tariffs on Canadian goods prompted the decision to hold a Canada-only event. The bridge remains on schedule despite the diplomatic snub.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall of their booked holiday home in Wexford last weekend. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay with her partner and two children, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned from a family party. Upon their return, she noticed the washing machine running, raising suspicions that led to the discovery. Booking.com has suspended the property and launched an investigation into the incident.

stories.title: Braunau in Österreich: Hitlers Geburtshaus wird zu Polizeistation
stories.summary: Hitler’s birthplace in Braunau, Austria, has been converted into a police station to deter right-wing activity. The building, previously a school and later a care facility, stood vacant for 15 years before the state took ownership in 2017. After years of debate, officials say the new police presence will neutralize the site’s historical burden. The station opens as the latest chapter in the building’s contested legacy.

stories.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
stories.summary: Spain will host the 2030 World Cup alongside Portugal and Morocco, marking the tournament’s centennial with opening matches in Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay. The defending champions enter as favorites after beating Argentina 1-0 in the 2026 final. Host nations are yet to decide which stadium will stage the final, with Spain’s Bernabeu and Barcelona’s Camp Nou among contenders. FIFA confirmed the expanded 48-team format will continue.

stories.title: Wrexham to appoint Newcastle's Nickson as sporting director
stories.summary: Wrexham will appoint Steve Nickson as sporting director on September 2, bringing 15 years of recruitment experience from Newcastle United. The 61-year-old, who helped sign Miguel Almiron and Sven Botman, will work with manager Phil Parkinson as the club eyes Premier League promotion. Wrexham, owned by Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney, secured three straight promotions under Parkinson between 2023 and 2025. His role will focus on transfers and football operations as the club expands its ambitions.

stories.title: Man Utd news: £60m transfer target makes decision as star gets 'calls and texts' over move
stories.summary: Manchester United striker Joshua Zirkzee has agreed to join Juventus in a summer transfer worth up to £60 million. The 25-year-old Dutch forward, who has scored five goals in 56 appearances for United since 2024, will return to Serie A after two years in England. Juventus are set to finalize the deal following talks with his representatives. The move follows United’s recent signings of midfielders Andrey Santos and Youri Tielemans.

stories.title: Far Away, le anticipazioni delle puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio
stories.summary: The Turkish series Far Away (Uzak Şehir) returns Monday, July 20, 2026, at 4:15 PM on Canale 5 and streams on Mediaset Infinity. The week’s episodes, airing through Friday, follow Cihan’s escalating conflict with rival families over Kaya and Zerrin’s disappearance. On July 22, a mob storms the Albora villa as Cihan asserts control, while Zerrin’s escape attempt ends with a surprise proposal. Next week’s episodes will explore the fallout of the confrontation and Cihan’s growing authority.

stories.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
stories.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas welcomed their first daughter, Lola Belén, on July 21 at a Madrid hospital after 41 weeks of pregnancy. The couple, who reconnected at age 22, shared their excitement online ahead of the birth. Lola Belén joins the Pombo family, which grew in January with the birth of Mariana, daughter of María Pombo and Pablo Castellano. The newborn completes the couple’s transition to parenthood.

stories.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
stories.summary: FC Metz secured a convincing 4-1 victory over newly promoted Ligue 2 side FC Sochaux in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick while Jessy Deminguet added the fourth as Metz dominated early before holding firm in the second half. Sochaux pulled one back late through Mathis Clairicia. Metz’s strong performance follows two earlier wins in their summer schedule.

stories.title: Boyd found guilty of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas
stories.summary: Derek Boyd, 31, was convicted of murdering Mark Carroll, 34, in Finglas, Dublin, on Wednesday. Boyd, already serving a suspended sentence for manslaughter, fatally stabbed Carroll during a late-night argument at his home on June 9, 2024. He will be sentenced to life in prison on Tuesday. Boyd fled to Scotland hours after the killing but was later arrested and extradited.

stories.title: Paul Nardi, un renfort d’expérience pour l’AJA
stories.summary: Paul Nardi, 32 ans, rejoint l’AJ Auxerre pour deux saisons jusqu’en juin 2028. Le gardien, libre de tout contrat après son passage aux Queens Park Rangers, portera le numéro 1. Il apporte son expérience après le départ de Donovan Léon vers l’Arabie saoudite. Nardi, formé à Nancy et passé par Monaco, Lorient et la Belgique, a disputé près de 350 matchs professionnels.

stories.title: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam Prediction, Betting Tips, Lineups & Odds | 22 Jul 2026
stories.summary: Bodo/Glimt host HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday, July 22, at 10:00 in Eliteserien. Bodo/Glimt, third in the league, beat Fredrikstad 1-0 last Friday, extending their unbeaten run to six matches. HamKam, seventh, lost 4-1 to Tromsø last weekend. Bodo/Glimt are favored to win by at least three goals, with betting odds at -111 for the Asian handicap.

stories.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
stories.summary: A 16-year-old minor was arrested in Rennes on July 21, 2026, for the July 4 rape of an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building’s lobby. The suspect, a homeless unaccompanied foreigner, was found with cocaine during his arrest and identified via DNA. He will be brought before prosecutors on July 24. Authorities continue investigating the case.

stories.title: Avs, Ilaria Salis condannata per aver licenziato due collaboratori senza giusta causa
stories.summary: Il Tribunale di Milano ha condannato l'eurodeputata Ilaria Salis a risarcire oltre 300mila euro per il licenziamento senza giusta causa di due collaboratori nel febbraio 2025. I contratti, in scadenza nel luglio 2029, prevedevano mansioni strategiche e retribuzioni analoghe. Salis, eletta con Avs nel giugno 2024, ha presentato ricorso in appello. La sentenza, emessa a marzo, ha riconosciuto 300.900 euro per i mancati guadagni e 10mila per le spese legali.

stories.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
stories.summary: Matthis Abline, 23 ans, s’apprête à quitter le FC Nantes pour l’AS Monaco dans un transfert record estimé à 30 millions d’euros. L’attaquant, sous contrat jusqu’en 2028, a déjà validé son départ après une saison difficile (6 buts en Ligue 1). Monaco devance la concurrence, dont Galatasaray, et finalise les détails avant l’officialisation. Le club monégasque mise sur son profil pour renforcer son attaque.

stories.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
stories.summary: Henrique Coelho, 26, died on 20 January 2024 at Galway University Hospital after being discharged two days earlier despite symptoms including chest pain and abnormal test results. The hospital issued a formal apology to his family, who received €35,000 in damages at the High Court. Coelho had been admitted on 18 January 2024 with shortness of breath and was diagnosed with a lower respiratory tract infection before his death. A postmortem examination followed.

stories.title: Asyl-Beratungsmobil fährt seit Februar durch den Landkreis
stories.summary: A mobile counseling unit has been operating in the Weilheim-Schongau district since February, offering on-site asylum advice. In 14 stops, it has advised around 70 people. The converted vehicle, funded by Bavaria’s integration budget, serves as a confidential workspace for state-supported advisors. The current schedule is available online.

stories.title: L'Inter punta Romero! Costa tanto ma è in rotta con il Tottenham. E c'è l'amico Lautaro che...
stories.summary: Inter targets Tottenham defender Cristian Romero to bolster its defense ahead of the new Serie A season. The Argentine defender, who played in Italy for Atalanta from 2019 to 2022, could return to San Siro on August 22. Romero, a key figure in Argentina’s World Cup and Copa América wins, has seen his market value rise since moving to England in 2022. The club aims to finalize a deal before the transfer window closes.

stories.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
stories.summary: Tyson Fury faces Mariusz Wach in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, July 24, with just 1,500 fans in attendance. The former heavyweight champion, 37, aims to secure a potential showdown with Anthony Joshua after his warm-up fight in Saudi Arabia. The bout won’t be broadcast live, leaving fans unable to assess Fury’s form ahead of the proposed November rematch. A victory would set up the long-awaited Fury-Joshua clash.

stories.title: Bohs look to lay Dalymount platform against Ballkani
stories.summary: Bohemians host Ballkani of Kosovo in the UEFA Conference League second-round qualifier first leg at Dalymount Park at 6pm. Bohs beat St Joseph’s 2-0 in the opener before a 0-0 draw in Gibraltar secured at least €700,000 in prize money. Ballkani edged past Connah’s Quay Nomads 3-2 after a nervy second leg in Pristina. Bohs, second in the League of Ireland, aim to build on their European progress against a Kosovo side braced for a tougher test.

stories.title: NEW SIGNING INTERVIEW: Filip Bilbija
stories.summary: Derby County have signed versatile German attacker Filip Bilbija on a three-year deal as a free agent. The 26-year-old joins after his contract with SC Paderborn expired, following 17 goals in all competitions last season. Bilbija, who can play as an attacking midfielder, winger or striker, has already linked up with the Rams in Austria. He could feature in Friday’s friendly against Freiburg as Derby continue pre-season preparations.

stories.title: Podemos denuncia que el Gobierno quiere "colar" la fallida reforma de la ley del suelo en el decreto de alquileres
stories.summary: El Gobierno incluirá en el decreto de alquileres una reforma de la ley del suelo rechazada en 2024, lo que ha generado un conflicto con Podemos. La formación exige la retirada de estos artículos como condición para negociar, mientras el PNV los considera clave para agilizar planeamientos urbanísticos. El decreto, que prorrogará contratos de alquiler hasta dos años, se votará en septiembre. La pugna amenaza con retrasar su aprobación.

stories.title: Miljoenenverkoop Cissoko keert drie jaar na vertrek bij NEC terug in Eredivisie
stories.summary: Ibrahim Cissoko keert terug in de Eredivisie bij PEC Zwolle. De 23-jarige linksbuiten, die drie jaar geleden voor drie miljoen euro vertrok bij NEC, tekent een contract tot medio 2030. Na een teleurstellend avontuur in Frankrijk met verhuurperiodes in Engeland, hoopt hij in Zwolle weer zijn vorm te hervinden. PEC Zwolle versterkt zo zijn aanvalslinie met een ervaren vleugelaanvaller.

stories.title: Montanelli e l'ossessione di farsi capire: la sua lezione a 25 anni dalla morte
stories.summary: Twenty-five years after his death on July 22, 2001, Fucecchio honored journalist Indro Montanelli with a ceremony at his municipal cemetery urn. Mayor Emma Donnini and Foundation president Letizia Moizzi laid flowers beside his ashes. Montanelli remains a defining figure in Italian journalism. His sharp commentary still shapes how many view today’s political debates.

stories.title: Wie verstärkt sich der BVB nach den Abgängen von Brandt und Adeyemi?
stories.summary: Borussia Dortmund has signed midfielder Joao Gadou (19) from Salzburg for €19.5 million as their first major summer transfer. The club expects to finalize Karim Adeyemi’s €22 million move from Barcelona within days, pending official announcements. Three other new players have already joined, but further signings are needed after Julian Brandt’s departure. Dortmund’s transfer activity is set to accelerate as the Bundesliga transfer window approaches.

stories.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
stories.summary: Alessandro Bastoni remains Inter Milan’s priority as the club’s summer transfer focus, with no concrete offers from Al-Hilal despite reported interest. The Saudi club cannot sign a new foreign player until offloading one, delaying any potential move. Inter’s defensive needs and Bastoni’s contract status keep him inedible for now, though a record bid could force reconsideration. His future hinges on negotiations expected to unfold in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
stories.summary: A skûtsje from Joure collided with a crowd on a steiger during a Friesland sailing race on Friday, sending spectators scrambling. No injuries were reported, but the skûtsje sustained minor damage after veering off course when wind shifts altered the race. The SKS is reviewing safety rules to prevent similar incidents next year. Officials say the narrow finish channel contributed to the close-quarters mishaps.

stories.title: Preview: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam - prediction, team news, lineups
stories.summary: Bodo/Glimt host HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday in Eliteserien. The hosts sit third, two points behind Tromso, after going unbeaten in their last six league matches. HamKam, seventh, have lost three straight games, including a 4-1 defeat to Tromso. Bodo/Glimt have conceded just 11 goals all season, the fewest in the league.

stories.title: 10 clubs paid the most by FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Manchester City received $4.4 million from FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup, the highest payout among clubs. Barcelona and Bayern Munich followed with $3.9 million and $3.4 million respectively. FIFA distributed a total of $355 million to clubs, a 70% increase from previous tournaments. Clubs earned around $5,000 per player per day based on their involvement.

stories.title: Heathrow switched to single runway operations following British Airways BA547 emergency landing
stories.summary: A British Airways flight from Rome declared an emergency over a landing gear hydraulic failure, forcing a go-around at Heathrow. The Airbus A320 aborted its approach at 450 feet, climbed to 3,700 feet, and entered a holding pattern for 20 minutes. Air traffic control confirmed the aircraft later landed safely in Liverpool. The incident briefly disrupted Heathrow’s operations, shifting to single-runway use during the emergency.

stories.title: Cyril Hanouna et Shana Loustau en couple ? "Il n'y a plus de doute", cette vidéo de leur prestigieux dîner fait bondir
stories.summary: Cyril Hanouna and Shana Loustau were spotted together at a high-profile charity gala in Saint-Tropez on July 21, 2026, fueling fresh rumors of a romantic relationship. The event, hosted by Cathy Guetta at Nikki Beach Saint-Tropez, featured a luxury auction raising funds for sickle cell disease research. Neither Hanouna nor Loustau has confirmed the relationship, though Loustau recently announced her split from her former partner. Further public appearances may clarify their status.

stories.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
stories.summary: Harry Potter keert voor het laatst tijdelijk terug naar de Nederlandse bioscopen. Pathé organiseert van 26 augustus tot 2 september een speciale speelweek ter ere van het 25-jarig jubileum van de eerste film, met een 24-uurs marathon in de nacht van 29 op 30 augustus. Kaartverkoop start op 31 juli, de verjaardag van Harry Potter. De marathon vindt plaats in elf geselecteerde bioscopen.

stories.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
stories.summary: Max Wöber has left Leeds United after agreeing to mutually terminate his contract. The Austrian defender, who joined in 2023, made 19 appearances in his first season before moving to Werder Bremen on loan. He is now set to join Bundesliga side Schalke on a free transfer after completing a medical. The 28-year-old departs Elland Road with fewer than 30 first-team appearances.

stories.title: French Jewish DJ Barbara Butch is defiant after concert disrupted by pro-Palestinian protesters
stories.summary: French Jewish DJ Barbara Butch faced a pro-Palestinian protest at her Paris concert on May 18, disrupting the event. Authorities intervened as protesters clashed with security, leading to multiple arrests. Butch, known for her activism, condemned the disruption while emphasizing her commitment to her performance. Authorities are investigating the incident and reviewing security measures for future events.

stories.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
stories.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía is apologizing to her Argentine fans after facing backlash for reposting a controversial Instagram Story following Spain's victory over Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19. The repost featured social media personality Mia Khalifa, who mocked Argentina's defeat, prompting outrage among fans. Rosalía, who is scheduled to perform in Buenos Aires on August 1, clarified that she did not read the accompanying text before sharing and expressed her love for Argentina in her apology.

stories.title: Waterford man, 36, charged over suspected stabbing at Tramore church
stories.summary: A 36-year-old Waterford man has been charged with possession of a knife and assault causing harm to a woman in her 50s at Holy Cross Church in Tramore, Co Waterford. The incident occurred on a date not specified in the report. Gardaí are continuing their investigation into the suspected stabbing. Further details are expected to be revealed during the court proceedings.

stories.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
stories.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess potential health factors. Authorities say Fakir was in a confused state when officers intervened. The incident has reignited debates over legal protections for police actions.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: The Committee on Authorisation to Proceed has rejected the request by prosecutors at the Rome Public Prosecutor’s Office to obtain the chat records between Andrea Delmastro and the restaurateur Mauro Caroccia , who is in custody on mafia charges. Meanwhile, MPs from the Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in the Senate in protest, holding up placards reading ‘release the chat logs’. The sitting was suspended. The occupation of the government benches lasted over half an hour. “Shame on you, shame on you,” shouted the Five Star Movement senators. The centre-right responded in unison: “Clowns, clowns”.

stories.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
stories.summary: The PSOE has reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero amid new allegations linking him to the public rescue of Plus Ultra Airlines. Party spokesperson Montse Mínguez stated that the judicial investigation will clarify the situation, emphasizing confidence in Zapatero's claims of lawful conduct. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas highlighted the need for factual defenses as the investigation continues, underscoring the importance of transparency in the ongoing case.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Op de Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam zijn twee trams met elkaar in botsing gekomen, waarbij een van de trams is ontspoord. Volgens de veiligheidsregio zijn er vijftien mensen gewond geraakt, van wie één zwaargewond. Vijf mensen zijn naar het ziekenhuis gebracht. De overige gewonden zijn ter plaatse behandeld door ambulancemedewerkers en konden daarna naar huis. De Rotterdamse burgemeester Carola Schouten bezocht de Erasmusbrug en spreekt van "een vreselijk ongeluk". "Het is een enorme klap geweest", aldus Schouten. "Er zijn gewonden gevallen.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at his family’s Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family across New Jersey and Arizona. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements scheduled.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: The Guardian view on Andy Burnham and devolution: it takes a powerful centre to decentralise power | Editorial
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has signaled his intent to strengthen the central government's power in London, despite his previous pledge to decentralize power. This move comes as the UK's devolved administrations, such as the one in Manchester, have limited control over fiscal policy. The Treasury has historically been cautious about granting more autonomy to local governments. The prime minister's office is also under-resourced compared to similar institutions in other democracies.

stories.title: How Long Can ‘The Odyssey’ Stay on Imax 70mm Screens? Nolan Fans Scramble as New Showtimes Sell Out 7 Weeks in Advance
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey has sold out IMAX 70mm screenings through mid-August in New York and Los Angeles, and through early September in London. The film’s exclusive 70mm format runs in just 25 U.S. and 41 global theaters. Additional showtimes will be added, but demand remains intense ahead of the August 14 release of The End of Oak Street. The next major IMAX 70mm commitment is Dune: Part Three, meaning The Odyssey won’t be forced off screens until then.

stories.title: Ex-Man Utd midfielder Casemiro joins Inter Miami
stories.summary: Former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro has signed with Inter Miami on a deal running to the end of the 2027 season, with an option to extend until June 2029. The 34-year-old Brazilian international joins the Major League Soccer side after making 160 appearances for United, scoring 26 goals and providing 14 assists. Casemiro won five Champions League titles with Real Madrid before moving to United in 2022. He will now play for Inter Miami, a team co-owned by Sir David Beckham.

stories.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
stories.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's feature directorial debut, "Ali G: Who Iz I?", is set for a theatrical release on October 23. The comedy film follows Cohen's character Ali G as he interviews people on the street, often getting into humorous and cringe-worthy situations. Cohen originated the Ali G character in 1998 and reprised it in various projects. The film marks a return to the character after a nearly two-decade absence.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely include more RAM, possibly 12GB, compared to the original 8GB. The updated model may also feature new colors and a refreshed design. Component shortages are expected to drive up the cost of the next Neo. Apple is also planning to release new iMacs with OLED screens, following a 2024 refresh.

stories.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
stories.summary: Natalie Fleet has stepped down as safeguarding minister, citing the role as "overwhelmingly triggering" due to her own experiences of grooming and rape as a teenager. She was appointed to the role on May 12 this year. Fleet says she made the decision to prioritize her health, describing it as the "bravest thing I've ever done". Her successor will now take on the responsibility of supporting victims.

stories.title: Philipsen sprints to stage 17 win
stories.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France, his first stage victory in the three-week race, with a bunch sprint finish. The 28-year-old Alpecin-Premier Tech rider powered over the line with a good gap over his rivals. Philipsen was aided by a powerful lead-out from Mathieu van der Poel, his team-mate. He will now look to close the gap on current green jersey wearer Mads Pedersen.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: A U.S.-Saudi nuclear cooperation agreement to develop a civilian nuclear program has raised concerns in Israel over a potential regional arms race. The deal, expected to be announced soon, would allow Saudi Arabia to enrich its own nuclear fuel. Israeli officials worry this could lead to nuclear proliferation, especially as Saudi Arabia has not ruled out pursuing nuclear weapons. Analysts suggest the agreement may encourage other countries, such as Turkey, to pursue similar nuclear capabilities.

stories.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
stories.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and his crewmates are concluding a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew will undock from the station's Rassvet module at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26, and land in Kazakhstan. They will have completed 3,856 orbits and traveled over 102 million miles. The crew is expected to fly by helicopter to Karaganda, Kazakhstan, after landing.

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8 in 100 balls, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and Lauren Bell, who picked up key wickets. The win marks a hard-fought start to the Brave's campaign.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to reduce obesity in Britain. The committee's findings are based on decades of flawed nutritional science that prioritized low-fat and calorie-controlled diets. The government is expected to implement new policies to address the issue.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and awaiting trial. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and their lawyers requested extradition to Russia. The Angolan authorities have now taken control of the case.

stories.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off on his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing a lack of authority to do so. Mamdani had previously called for Netanyahu's arrest over an International Criminal Court warrant for alleged war crimes. The US, where Netanyahu is expected to visit, is not an ICC member state, making an arrest unlikely. Mamdani now urges US officials to act on the ICC warrant.

stories.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
stories.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged with the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African expat Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors say the investigation, opened in May 2025, led to formal charges after initial custody and release in 2025. Carter’s husband, Alan, called her death traumatic for the family. Authorities continue to examine evidence as the case proceeds.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side that tested positive for live legionella bacteria. Testing is ongoing for the remaining 140 cooling tower systems. The city's health department is urging caution as new cases may still be investigated and confirmed.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: Incendio collina Monserrato a Modica, la Polizia. abbiamo arrestato l’autore del rogo
stories.summary: A 23-year-old foreign national was arrested in Modica on suspicion of starting wildfires that threatened the city. Police and firefighters worked together to identify and detain the suspect near Monserrato Hill. He faces charges of attempted arson and is under house arrest pending further investigation. Authorities continue to assess the damage and prevent further blazes.

offbeat.title: Spain's Fabian Ruiz, Gavi Get Their Weight In Tomatoes After World Cup Win
offbeat.summary: Getting showered with tomatoes after a performance is usually a bad thing. But in Spain, it's a sign of ultimate respect for two players in its World Cup-winning squad. Fabián Ruiz and Gavi were presented with their weight in tomatoes by their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca in the Andalusia region, located in the south of Spain. The two players joined former Spain great Jesus Navas (part of the 2010 World Cup squad) at the town's city hall as part of the ongoing revelry to celebrate La Roja's victory over Argentina in the final on Sunday. Andalusia is known for its agriculture and production of tomatoes.

offbeat.title: The night Lionel Messi got a jersey from Texas A&M QB Marcel Reed
offbeat.summary: Lionel Messi received a signed Texas A&M jersey from quarterback Marcel Reed during an exhibition match against Honduras on June 6, 2023, at Kyle Field, which drew over 90,000 fans. The brief exchange occurred after Argentina's 2-0 victory, with Reed also obtaining a signed ball from Messi. This moment highlights Messi's continued influence in sports, even as he prepares for a break following the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where Argentina faced a heartbreaking loss in the final.

offbeat.title: Modeling agent who introduced women to Jeffrey Epstein found dead in France
offbeat.summary: Daniel Siad, a 69-year-old modeling agent linked to Jeffrey Epstein, was found dead at his home in Colombes, France, on Monday. French prosecutors have opened an investigation into the cause of death, with an autopsy scheduled to be conducted. Siad was under scrutiny for allegedly facilitating Epstein's trafficking of young women and had denied any wrongdoing prior to his death. His name appeared in over 1,000 documents related to Epstein's case, raising significant concerns about his involvement.

offbeat.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
offbeat.summary: Four juveniles face court in Virginia Thursday after a serious public order incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, left three people with significant but non-life-threatening injuries. Police arrested the teens following searches near Lough Ramor after reports of a weapon being produced. The minors will appear before Cavan District Children's Court, with media barred from naming them. Investigations continue.

offbeat.title: The video of the Argentina national team in the tunnel that buries the conspiracy theories about the defeat against Spain
offbeat.summary: Argentina released unseen tunnel footage showing Messi rallying teammates before their 2026 World Cup final loss to Spain, dispelling online conspiracy theories about prearranged outcomes. The three-minute video, filmed at MetLife Stadium on July 19, 2026, captures Messi urging calm amid pressure. Meanwhile, Argentina marked the 30th anniversary of the 1994 AMIA bombing that killed 85, with U.S. lawmakers renewing calls for justice. Messi’s World Cup performance keeps him in Ballon d’Or contention through July 31.

offbeat.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
offbeat.summary: Democracy Dies in Darkness Jasper Philipsen has capped a fine team performance to win stage 17 of the Tour de France, where defending champion Tadej Pogačar protected his overall lead July 22, 2026 at 1:33 p.m. EDT 4 minutes ago Belgium’s Jasper Philipsen reacts after winning the seventeenth stage of the Tour de France cycling race, with start in Chambery and finish in Voiron France, Wednesday, July 22, 2026. (Gonzalo Fuentes/Pool Photo via AP) By Associated Press VOIRON, France — Jasper Philipsen capped a fine team performance Wednesday to win stage 17 of the Tour de France , where defending champion Tadej Pogačar protected his overall lead.

offbeat.title: Aston Villa agree deal to sign Chelsea forward Alejandro Garnacho
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa has secured a deal to sign Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho on an initial season-long loan, with a conditional obligation to make the transfer permanent. The 22-year-old has completed his medical and is finalizing paperwork for a four-year contract. Villa manager Unai Emery, a long-time admirer of Garnacho, aims to bolster his squad following Chelsea's record £117 million signing of Morgan Rogers from Villa. If certain conditions are met, the deal could become permanent by 2027.

offbeat.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
offbeat.summary: ResilExec Coaching has launched two new programs, Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™, aimed at mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. These initiatives complete a comprehensive career advancement platform, addressing critical moments where leaders often lose momentum, such as external transitions and post-advancement challenges. Founder Brian Baldari emphasizes that the programs will help leaders navigate the complexities of market re-entry and long-term career execution. The launch signifies a significant expansion of ResilExec's offerings to support professionals facing career shifts.

offbeat.title: No Garnacho for Roma & Napoli as Argentine joins Aston Villa on loan
offbeat.summary: Alejandro Garnacho is set to join Aston Villa on loan from Chelsea, with a conditional obligation to buy that could make the deal permanent in 2027. The agreement comes after interest from Serie A clubs Roma and Napoli, which was hindered by Garnacho's wage demands. Villa manager Unai Emery played a crucial role in the move, as the club aims to bolster its attacking options. Garnacho has already completed his medical and is finalizing paperwork in Birmingham.

offbeat.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
offbeat.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as they target a summer signing. The France international, who played one World Cup game, scored seven goals and made 11 assists last season. PSG have also bid for him, with Monaco rejecting their latest offer around £34 million. Liverpool are expected to make more additions before the transfer window closes.

offbeat.title: Forest Green Rovers make double swoop as midfielder and attacker join
offbeat.summary: Forest Green Rovers have made a double signing with forward Omari Patrick arriving on a permanent deal and midfielder Kyle Kelly on a season-long loan from Liverpool. Kelly, 20, has been in Liverpool's age-grade set-up since 2018, most recently playing for their under-21s team. The youngster also has eight caps fro St Kitts and Nevis, scoring once, during a friendly in March against the Solomon Islands. The loan is for an initial six months, and has left boss Robbie Savage delighted to have got his man. "What a great guy," he said. "Very excited to have Kyle join us at FGR.

offbeat.title: Cape Verde hero Vozinha features in FIFA's Best XI from World Cup, with three World Champions from Spain
offbeat.summary: FIFA has released the official World Cup Best XI, the best players chosen (voted by fans) for every position after the competition ended. Spain, world champions, feature three players, and Argentina, runner-ups, feature two.What surprised some is that France, who ended in fourth place after a thrilling 6-4 defeat to England in the third-place play-off, also has three players; while England only one (and is not Harry Kane).

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 - Schedule, draw and prize money
offbeat.summary: The 2026 World Matchplay Darts begins Saturday, July 18 at Blackpool’s Winter Gardens with defending champion Luke Littler leading the 32-player field. The PDC major event runs through July 26, including a women’s final on the last day where Lisa Ashton defends her title. Littler, the world number one, has already claimed three majors this year. The tournament features seeded draws to separate top-ranked players early on.

offbeat.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
offbeat.summary: New Jersey officials reported a software error registering over 6,600 non-U.S. citizens as voters ahead of the 2024 presidential election. Governor Mikie Sherrill called the mistake unacceptable and said fewer than 400 actually cast ballots. The error occurred between June 2023 and June 2024 during driver’s license and ID applications. State officials are reviewing safeguards ahead of upcoming elections.

offbeat.title: Text of new Gordie Howe bridge deal seems to contradict Carney
offbeat.summary: Canada will open the $4.7 billion Gordie Howe International Bridge on July 27, but a planned joint U.S.-Canada ceremony on July 24 has been canceled amid rising trade tensions. Ottawa released the text of a controversial revenue-sharing deal, promising to split net bridge profits with a U.S. economic fund for 15 years. President Trump’s new tariffs on Canadian goods prompted the decision to hold a Canada-only event. The bridge remains on schedule despite the diplomatic snub.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall of their booked holiday home in Wexford last weekend. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay with her partner and two children, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned from a family party. Upon their return, she noticed the washing machine running, raising suspicions that led to the discovery. Booking.com has suspended the property and launched an investigation into the incident.

offbeat.title: Braunau in Österreich: Hitlers Geburtshaus wird zu Polizeistation
offbeat.summary: Hitler’s birthplace in Braunau, Austria, has been converted into a police station to deter right-wing activity. The building, previously a school and later a care facility, stood vacant for 15 years before the state took ownership in 2017. After years of debate, officials say the new police presence will neutralize the site’s historical burden. The station opens as the latest chapter in the building’s contested legacy.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
offbeat.summary: Spain will host the 2030 World Cup alongside Portugal and Morocco, marking the tournament’s centennial with opening matches in Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay. The defending champions enter as favorites after beating Argentina 1-0 in the 2026 final. Host nations are yet to decide which stadium will stage the final, with Spain’s Bernabeu and Barcelona’s Camp Nou among contenders. FIFA confirmed the expanded 48-team format will continue.

offbeat.title: Wrexham to appoint Newcastle's Nickson as sporting director
offbeat.summary: Wrexham will appoint Steve Nickson as sporting director on September 2, bringing 15 years of recruitment experience from Newcastle United. The 61-year-old, who helped sign Miguel Almiron and Sven Botman, will work with manager Phil Parkinson as the club eyes Premier League promotion. Wrexham, owned by Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney, secured three straight promotions under Parkinson between 2023 and 2025. His role will focus on transfers and football operations as the club expands its ambitions.

offbeat.title: Man Utd news: £60m transfer target makes decision as star gets 'calls and texts' over move
offbeat.summary: Manchester United striker Joshua Zirkzee has agreed to join Juventus in a summer transfer worth up to £60 million. The 25-year-old Dutch forward, who has scored five goals in 56 appearances for United since 2024, will return to Serie A after two years in England. Juventus are set to finalize the deal following talks with his representatives. The move follows United’s recent signings of midfielders Andrey Santos and Youri Tielemans.

offbeat.title: Far Away, le anticipazioni delle puntate dal 20 al 24 luglio
offbeat.summary: The Turkish series Far Away (Uzak Şehir) returns Monday, July 20, 2026, at 4:15 PM on Canale 5 and streams on Mediaset Infinity. The week’s episodes, airing through Friday, follow Cihan’s escalating conflict with rival families over Kaya and Zerrin’s disappearance. On July 22, a mob storms the Albora villa as Cihan asserts control, while Zerrin’s escape attempt ends with a surprise proposal. Next week’s episodes will explore the fallout of the confrontation and Cihan’s growing authority.

offbeat.title: Lucía Pombo y Álvaro López-Huertas ya son padres de su primera hija
offbeat.summary: Lucía Pombo and Álvaro López-Huertas welcomed their first daughter, Lola Belén, on July 21 at a Madrid hospital after 41 weeks of pregnancy. The couple, who reconnected at age 22, shared their excitement online ahead of the birth. Lola Belén joins the Pombo family, which grew in January with the birth of Mariana, daughter of María Pombo and Pablo Castellano. The newborn completes the couple’s transition to parenthood.

offbeat.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
offbeat.summary: FC Metz secured a convincing 4-1 victory over newly promoted Ligue 2 side FC Sochaux in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick while Jessy Deminguet added the fourth as Metz dominated early before holding firm in the second half. Sochaux pulled one back late through Mathis Clairicia. Metz’s strong performance follows two earlier wins in their summer schedule.

offbeat.title: Boyd found guilty of murdering Mark Carroll in Finglas
offbeat.summary: Derek Boyd, 31, was convicted of murdering Mark Carroll, 34, in Finglas, Dublin, on Wednesday. Boyd, already serving a suspended sentence for manslaughter, fatally stabbed Carroll during a late-night argument at his home on June 9, 2024. He will be sentenced to life in prison on Tuesday. Boyd fled to Scotland hours after the killing but was later arrested and extradited.

offbeat.title: Paul Nardi, un renfort d’expérience pour l’AJA
offbeat.summary: Paul Nardi, 32 ans, rejoint l’AJ Auxerre pour deux saisons jusqu’en juin 2028. Le gardien, libre de tout contrat après son passage aux Queens Park Rangers, portera le numéro 1. Il apporte son expérience après le départ de Donovan Léon vers l’Arabie saoudite. Nardi, formé à Nancy et passé par Monaco, Lorient et la Belgique, a disputé près de 350 matchs professionnels.

offbeat.title: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam Prediction, Betting Tips, Lineups & Odds | 22 Jul 2026
offbeat.summary: Bodo/Glimt host HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday, July 22, at 10:00 in Eliteserien. Bodo/Glimt, third in the league, beat Fredrikstad 1-0 last Friday, extending their unbeaten run to six matches. HamKam, seventh, lost 4-1 to Tromsø last weekend. Bodo/Glimt are favored to win by at least three goals, with betting odds at -111 for the Asian handicap.

offbeat.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
offbeat.summary: A 16-year-old minor was arrested in Rennes on July 21, 2026, for the July 4 rape of an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building’s lobby. The suspect, a homeless unaccompanied foreigner, was found with cocaine during his arrest and identified via DNA. He will be brought before prosecutors on July 24. Authorities continue investigating the case.

offbeat.title: Avs, Ilaria Salis condannata per aver licenziato due collaboratori senza giusta causa
offbeat.summary: Il Tribunale di Milano ha condannato l'eurodeputata Ilaria Salis a risarcire oltre 300mila euro per il licenziamento senza giusta causa di due collaboratori nel febbraio 2025. I contratti, in scadenza nel luglio 2029, prevedevano mansioni strategiche e retribuzioni analoghe. Salis, eletta con Avs nel giugno 2024, ha presentato ricorso in appello. La sentenza, emessa a marzo, ha riconosciuto 300.900 euro per i mancati guadagni e 10mila per le spese legali.

offbeat.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
offbeat.summary: Matthis Abline, 23 ans, s’apprête à quitter le FC Nantes pour l’AS Monaco dans un transfert record estimé à 30 millions d’euros. L’attaquant, sous contrat jusqu’en 2028, a déjà validé son départ après une saison difficile (6 buts en Ligue 1). Monaco devance la concurrence, dont Galatasaray, et finalise les détails avant l’officialisation. Le club monégasque mise sur son profil pour renforcer son attaque.

offbeat.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
offbeat.summary: Henrique Coelho, 26, died on 20 January 2024 at Galway University Hospital after being discharged two days earlier despite symptoms including chest pain and abnormal test results. The hospital issued a formal apology to his family, who received €35,000 in damages at the High Court. Coelho had been admitted on 18 January 2024 with shortness of breath and was diagnosed with a lower respiratory tract infection before his death. A postmortem examination followed.

offbeat.title: Asyl-Beratungsmobil fährt seit Februar durch den Landkreis
offbeat.summary: A mobile counseling unit has been operating in the Weilheim-Schongau district since February, offering on-site asylum advice. In 14 stops, it has advised around 70 people. The converted vehicle, funded by Bavaria’s integration budget, serves as a confidential workspace for state-supported advisors. The current schedule is available online.

offbeat.title: L'Inter punta Romero! Costa tanto ma è in rotta con il Tottenham. E c'è l'amico Lautaro che...
offbeat.summary: Inter targets Tottenham defender Cristian Romero to bolster its defense ahead of the new Serie A season. The Argentine defender, who played in Italy for Atalanta from 2019 to 2022, could return to San Siro on August 22. Romero, a key figure in Argentina’s World Cup and Copa América wins, has seen his market value rise since moving to England in 2022. The club aims to finalize a deal before the transfer window closes.

offbeat.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
offbeat.summary: Tyson Fury faces Mariusz Wach in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, July 24, with just 1,500 fans in attendance. The former heavyweight champion, 37, aims to secure a potential showdown with Anthony Joshua after his warm-up fight in Saudi Arabia. The bout won’t be broadcast live, leaving fans unable to assess Fury’s form ahead of the proposed November rematch. A victory would set up the long-awaited Fury-Joshua clash.

offbeat.title: Bohs look to lay Dalymount platform against Ballkani
offbeat.summary: Bohemians host Ballkani of Kosovo in the UEFA Conference League second-round qualifier first leg at Dalymount Park at 6pm. Bohs beat St Joseph’s 2-0 in the opener before a 0-0 draw in Gibraltar secured at least €700,000 in prize money. Ballkani edged past Connah’s Quay Nomads 3-2 after a nervy second leg in Pristina. Bohs, second in the League of Ireland, aim to build on their European progress against a Kosovo side braced for a tougher test.

offbeat.title: NEW SIGNING INTERVIEW: Filip Bilbija
offbeat.summary: Derby County have signed versatile German attacker Filip Bilbija on a three-year deal as a free agent. The 26-year-old joins after his contract with SC Paderborn expired, following 17 goals in all competitions last season. Bilbija, who can play as an attacking midfielder, winger or striker, has already linked up with the Rams in Austria. He could feature in Friday’s friendly against Freiburg as Derby continue pre-season preparations.

offbeat.title: Podemos denuncia que el Gobierno quiere "colar" la fallida reforma de la ley del suelo en el decreto de alquileres
offbeat.summary: El Gobierno incluirá en el decreto de alquileres una reforma de la ley del suelo rechazada en 2024, lo que ha generado un conflicto con Podemos. La formación exige la retirada de estos artículos como condición para negociar, mientras el PNV los considera clave para agilizar planeamientos urbanísticos. El decreto, que prorrogará contratos de alquiler hasta dos años, se votará en septiembre. La pugna amenaza con retrasar su aprobación.

offbeat.title: Miljoenenverkoop Cissoko keert drie jaar na vertrek bij NEC terug in Eredivisie
offbeat.summary: Ibrahim Cissoko keert terug in de Eredivisie bij PEC Zwolle. De 23-jarige linksbuiten, die drie jaar geleden voor drie miljoen euro vertrok bij NEC, tekent een contract tot medio 2030. Na een teleurstellend avontuur in Frankrijk met verhuurperiodes in Engeland, hoopt hij in Zwolle weer zijn vorm te hervinden. PEC Zwolle versterkt zo zijn aanvalslinie met een ervaren vleugelaanvaller.

offbeat.title: Montanelli e l'ossessione di farsi capire: la sua lezione a 25 anni dalla morte
offbeat.summary: Twenty-five years after his death on July 22, 2001, Fucecchio honored journalist Indro Montanelli with a ceremony at his municipal cemetery urn. Mayor Emma Donnini and Foundation president Letizia Moizzi laid flowers beside his ashes. Montanelli remains a defining figure in Italian journalism. His sharp commentary still shapes how many view today’s political debates.

offbeat.title: Wie verstärkt sich der BVB nach den Abgängen von Brandt und Adeyemi?
offbeat.summary: Borussia Dortmund has signed midfielder Joao Gadou (19) from Salzburg for €19.5 million as their first major summer transfer. The club expects to finalize Karim Adeyemi’s €22 million move from Barcelona within days, pending official announcements. Three other new players have already joined, but further signings are needed after Julian Brandt’s departure. Dortmund’s transfer activity is set to accelerate as the Bundesliga transfer window approaches.

offbeat.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
offbeat.summary: Alessandro Bastoni remains Inter Milan’s priority as the club’s summer transfer focus, with no concrete offers from Al-Hilal despite reported interest. The Saudi club cannot sign a new foreign player until offloading one, delaying any potential move. Inter’s defensive needs and Bastoni’s contract status keep him inedible for now, though a record bid could force reconsideration. His future hinges on negotiations expected to unfold in the coming weeks.

offbeat.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
offbeat.summary: A skûtsje from Joure collided with a crowd on a steiger during a Friesland sailing race on Friday, sending spectators scrambling. No injuries were reported, but the skûtsje sustained minor damage after veering off course when wind shifts altered the race. The SKS is reviewing safety rules to prevent similar incidents next year. Officials say the narrow finish channel contributed to the close-quarters mishaps.

offbeat.title: Preview: Bodo/Glimt vs HamKam - prediction, team news, lineups
offbeat.summary: Bodo/Glimt host HamKam at Aspmyra Stadion on Wednesday in Eliteserien. The hosts sit third, two points behind Tromso, after going unbeaten in their last six league matches. HamKam, seventh, have lost three straight games, including a 4-1 defeat to Tromso. Bodo/Glimt have conceded just 11 goals all season, the fewest in the league.

offbeat.title: 10 clubs paid the most by FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup
offbeat.summary: Manchester City received $4.4 million from FIFA for releasing players to the 2026 World Cup, the highest payout among clubs. Barcelona and Bayern Munich followed with $3.9 million and $3.4 million respectively. FIFA distributed a total of $355 million to clubs, a 70% increase from previous tournaments. Clubs earned around $5,000 per player per day based on their involvement.

offbeat.title: Heathrow switched to single runway operations following British Airways BA547 emergency landing
offbeat.summary: A British Airways flight from Rome declared an emergency over a landing gear hydraulic failure, forcing a go-around at Heathrow. The Airbus A320 aborted its approach at 450 feet, climbed to 3,700 feet, and entered a holding pattern for 20 minutes. Air traffic control confirmed the aircraft later landed safely in Liverpool. The incident briefly disrupted Heathrow’s operations, shifting to single-runway use during the emergency.

offbeat.title: Cyril Hanouna et Shana Loustau en couple ? "Il n'y a plus de doute", cette vidéo de leur prestigieux dîner fait bondir
offbeat.summary: Cyril Hanouna and Shana Loustau were spotted together at a high-profile charity gala in Saint-Tropez on July 21, 2026, fueling fresh rumors of a romantic relationship. The event, hosted by Cathy Guetta at Nikki Beach Saint-Tropez, featured a luxury auction raising funds for sickle cell disease research. Neither Hanouna nor Loustau has confirmed the relationship, though Loustau recently announced her split from her former partner. Further public appearances may clarify their status.

offbeat.title: Harry Potter keert voorlopig voor het laatst terug naar het grote doek: Pathé viert 25-jarig jubileum met 24-uurs marathon
offbeat.summary: Harry Potter keert voor het laatst tijdelijk terug naar de Nederlandse bioscopen. Pathé organiseert van 26 augustus tot 2 september een speciale speelweek ter ere van het 25-jarig jubileum van de eerste film, met een 24-uurs marathon in de nacht van 29 op 30 augustus. Kaartverkoop start op 31 juli, de verjaardag van Harry Potter. De marathon vindt plaats in elf geselecteerde bioscopen.

offbeat.title: Max Wöber leaves Leeds United
offbeat.summary: Max Wöber has left Leeds United after agreeing to mutually terminate his contract. The Austrian defender, who joined in 2023, made 19 appearances in his first season before moving to Werder Bremen on loan. He is now set to join Bundesliga side Schalke on a free transfer after completing a medical. The 28-year-old departs Elland Road with fewer than 30 first-team appearances.

offbeat.title: French Jewish DJ Barbara Butch is defiant after concert disrupted by pro-Palestinian protesters
offbeat.summary: French Jewish DJ Barbara Butch faced a pro-Palestinian protest at her Paris concert on May 18, disrupting the event. Authorities intervened as protesters clashed with security, leading to multiple arrests. Butch, known for her activism, condemned the disruption while emphasizing her commitment to her performance. Authorities are investigating the incident and reviewing security measures for future events.

offbeat.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
offbeat.summary: Spanish singer Rosalía is apologizing to her Argentine fans after facing backlash for reposting a controversial Instagram Story following Spain's victory over Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19. The repost featured social media personality Mia Khalifa, who mocked Argentina's defeat, prompting outrage among fans. Rosalía, who is scheduled to perform in Buenos Aires on August 1, clarified that she did not read the accompanying text before sharing and expressed her love for Argentina in her apology.

offbeat.title: Waterford man, 36, charged over suspected stabbing at Tramore church
offbeat.summary: A 36-year-old Waterford man has been charged with possession of a knife and assault causing harm to a woman in her 50s at Holy Cross Church in Tramore, Co Waterford. The incident occurred on a date not specified in the report. Gardaí are continuing their investigation into the suspected stabbing. Further details are expected to be revealed during the court proceedings.

offbeat.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
offbeat.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna after being restrained by police during a confrontation. An autopsy is underway to determine the cause of death and assess potential health factors. Authorities say Fakir was in a confused state when officers intervened. The incident has reignited debates over legal protections for police actions.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: The Committee on Authorisation to Proceed has rejected the request by prosecutors at the Rome Public Prosecutor’s Office to obtain the chat records between Andrea Delmastro and the restaurateur Mauro Caroccia , who is in custody on mafia charges. Meanwhile, MPs from the Five Star Movement occupied the government benches in the Senate in protest, holding up placards reading ‘release the chat logs’. The sitting was suspended. The occupation of the government benches lasted over half an hour. “Shame on you, shame on you,” shouted the Five Star Movement senators. The centre-right responded in unison: “Clowns, clowns”.

offbeat.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
offbeat.summary: The PSOE has reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero amid new allegations linking him to the public rescue of Plus Ultra Airlines. Party spokesperson Montse Mínguez stated that the judicial investigation will clarify the situation, emphasizing confidence in Zapatero's claims of lawful conduct. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas highlighted the need for factual defenses as the investigation continues, underscoring the importance of transparency in the ongoing case.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Op de Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam zijn twee trams met elkaar in botsing gekomen, waarbij een van de trams is ontspoord. Volgens de veiligheidsregio zijn er vijftien mensen gewond geraakt, van wie één zwaargewond. Vijf mensen zijn naar het ziekenhuis gebracht. De overige gewonden zijn ter plaatse behandeld door ambulancemedewerkers en konden daarna naar huis. De Rotterdamse burgemeester Carola Schouten bezocht de Erasmusbrug en spreekt van "een vreselijk ongeluk". "Het is een enorme klap geweest", aldus Schouten. "Er zijn gewonden gevallen.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at his family’s Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family across New Jersey and Arizona. Funeral services will be private, with no public arrangements scheduled.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

offbeat.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
offbeat.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and their lawyers requested extradition to Russia. The Angolan authorities have now taken control of the case.

offbeat.title: Mamdani backs off on pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has backed off on his pledge to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, citing a lack of authority to do so. Mamdani had previously called for Netanyahu's arrest over an International Criminal Court warrant for alleged war crimes. The US, where Netanyahu is expected to visit, is not an ICC member state, making an arrest unlikely. Mamdani now urges US officials to act on the ICC warrant.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman in France has been formally charged with the April 2024 stabbing death of British-South African expat Karen Carter, 65, in the village of Trémolat. Prosecutors say the investigation, opened in May 2025, led to formal charges after initial custody and release in 2025. Carter’s husband, Alan, called her death traumatic for the family. Authorities continue to examine evidence as the case proceeds.

offbeat.title: Incendio collina Monserrato a Modica, la Polizia. abbiamo arrestato l’autore del rogo
offbeat.summary: A 23-year-old foreign national was arrested in Modica on suspicion of starting wildfires that threatened the city. Police and firefighters worked together to identify and detain the suspect near Monserrato Hill. He faces charges of attempted arson and is under house arrest pending further investigation. Authorities continue to assess the damage and prevent further blazes.

politics.title: The Guardian view on Andy Burnham and devolution: it takes a powerful centre to decentralise power | Editorial
politics.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has signaled his intent to strengthen the central government's power in London, despite his previous pledge to decentralize power. This move comes as the UK's devolved administrations, such as the one in Manchester, have limited control over fiscal policy. The Treasury has historically been cautious about granting more autonomy to local governments. The prime minister's office is also under-resourced compared to similar institutions in other democracies.

politics.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
politics.summary: Natalie Fleet has stepped down as safeguarding minister, citing the role as "overwhelmingly triggering" due to her own experiences of grooming and rape as a teenager. She was appointed to the role on May 12 this year. Fleet says she made the decision to prioritize her health, describing it as the "bravest thing I've ever done". Her successor will now take on the responsibility of supporting victims.

politics.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
politics.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

entertainment.title: How Long Can ‘The Odyssey’ Stay on Imax 70mm Screens? Nolan Fans Scramble as New Showtimes Sell Out 7 Weeks in Advance
entertainment.summary: Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey has sold out IMAX 70mm screenings through mid-August in New York and Los Angeles, and through early September in London. The film’s exclusive 70mm format runs in just 25 U.S. and 41 global theaters. Additional showtimes will be added, but demand remains intense ahead of the August 14 release of The End of Oak Street. The next major IMAX 70mm commitment is Dune: Part Three, meaning The Odyssey won’t be forced off screens until then.

entertainment.title: Sacha Baron Cohen’s ‘Ali G: Who Iz I?’ Sets October Release From Amazon MGM
entertainment.summary: Sacha Baron Cohen's feature directorial debut, "Ali G: Who Iz I?", is set for a theatrical release on October 23. The comedy film follows Cohen's character Ali G as he interviews people on the street, often getting into humorous and cringe-worthy situations. Cohen originated the Ali G character in 1998 and reprised it in various projects. The film marks a return to the character after a nearly two-decade absence.

entertainment.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
entertainment.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series. The show follows a disgraced financier accused of massive fraud, navigating prison politics and awaiting trial. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

sports.title: Ex-Man Utd midfielder Casemiro joins Inter Miami
sports.summary: Former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro has signed with Inter Miami on a deal running to the end of the 2027 season, with an option to extend until June 2029. The 34-year-old Brazilian international joins the Major League Soccer side after making 160 appearances for United, scoring 26 goals and providing 14 assists. Casemiro won five Champions League titles with Real Madrid before moving to United in 2022. He will now play for Inter Miami, a team co-owned by Sir David Beckham.

sports.title: Philipsen sprints to stage 17 win
sports.summary: Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France, his first stage victory in the three-week race, with a bunch sprint finish. The 28-year-old Alpecin-Premier Tech rider powered over the line with a good gap over his rivals. Philipsen was aided by a powerful lead-out from Mathieu van der Poel, his team-mate. He will now look to close the gap on current green jersey wearer Mads Pedersen.

sports.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
