sports.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire replied with 118-8 in 100 balls, led by Georgia Voll's 52. The Brave's fightback was sparked by 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman and Lauren Bell, who picked up key wickets. The win marks a hard-fought start to the Brave's campaign.

tech.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
tech.summary: Apple is reportedly developing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely include more RAM, possibly 12GB, compared to the original 8GB. The updated model may also feature new colors and a refreshed design. Component shortages are expected to drive up the cost of the next Neo. Apple is also planning to release new iMacs with OLED screens, following a 2024 refresh.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

tech.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
tech.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
breaking.summary: A U.S.-Saudi nuclear cooperation agreement to develop a civilian nuclear program has raised concerns in Israel over a potential regional arms race. The deal, expected to be announced soon, would allow Saudi Arabia to enrich its own nuclear fuel. Israeli officials worry this could lead to nuclear proliferation, especially as Saudi Arabia has not ruled out pursuing nuclear weapons. Analysts suggest the agreement may encourage other countries, such as Turkey, to pursue similar nuclear capabilities.

breaking.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
breaking.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

breaking.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
breaking.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

facts.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
facts.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and his crewmates are concluding a 241-day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew will undock from the station's Rassvet module at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26, and land in Kazakhstan. They will have completed 3,856 orbits and traveled over 102 million miles. The crew is expected to fly by helicopter to Karaganda, Kazakhstan, after landing.

facts.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
facts.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report finds that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to reduce obesity in Britain. The committee's findings are based on decades of flawed nutritional science that prioritized low-fat and calorie-controlled diets. The government is expected to implement new policies to address the issue.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side that tested positive for live legionella bacteria. Testing is ongoing for the remaining 140 cooling tower systems. The city's health department is urging caution as new cases may still be investigated and confirmed.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
crisis.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.
